pred_reason: The SWC-101 label applies due to the integer underflow in SafeMath.ceil, which violates arithmetic safety. SWC-107 is appropriate because the reentrancy vulnerability in transferFrom breaks the checks-effects-interactions pattern. Evidence: Integer underflow occurs in SafeMath.ceil when subtracting 1 from overflowed values (Finding 2); transferFrom updates state after external calls via event emissions, enabling reentrancy attacks (Finding 1)

findings.Description: The transferFrom function updates state after potential external interactions (event emissions), violating checks-effects-interactions pattern. This appears in the smart contract code only and was identified during manual audit.
findings.Impact: Potential for attackers to drain funds through recursive calls before allowances are properly updated
findings.Location: transferFrom function, lines 118-135 in WenCoinbase contract

findings.Description: The ceil function can underflow when subtracting 1 from an overflowed value. This appears in the smart contract code only and was identified during manual audit.
findings.Impact: Denial of service for large token transfers, breaking core contract functionality
findings.Location: SafeMath.ceil function, line 49-52 in SafeMath library

findings.Description: The div function lacks a zero-division check, which could cause runtime reverts. This appears in the smart contract code only and was identified during manual audit.
findings.Impact: Transaction failures and potential denial of service if division by zero occurs
findings.Location: SafeMath.div function, line 33-36 in SafeMath library

findings.Issue: Lack of access controls for administrative functions
findings.Description: No ownership controls or administrative restrictions exist, making the contract inflexible and unable to respond to emergencies. This appears in the smart contract code only and was identified during manual audit.
findings.Impact: Cannot modify burn rate, pause transfers, or recover tokens; permanent rigid tokenomics
findings.Location: Contract structure - missing ownership pattern throughout WenCoinbase contract

findings.Description: Standard ERC-20 approve front-running vulnerability where spenders can use old allowances before updates. This appears in the smart contract code only and is a known ERC-20 issue.
findings.Impact: Spenders can exceed intended allowance limits by front-running allowance reduction transactions

findings.Issue: Precision loss and calculation issues in findOnePercent
findings.Description: The fee calculation may suffer from precision loss and uses confusing variable naming. This appears in the smart contract code only and was identified during manual audit.
findings.Impact: Incorrect token burning amounts, affecting token economics and supply
